passage.label: Amy
passage.text: I volunteer at an animal shelter every Saturday morning. I help to feed the dogs and clean their cages. It's hard work because some of the dogs are quite big and they need a lot of exercise, but I really love being with animals. I've learned so much about how to look after them properly, especially about their diet and health. My friends sometimes come with me, but they don't come every week because they have other activities. I think it's very important to help animals who have no home, and it makes me feel really good to know that I'm making a small difference in their lives.

passage.label: Ben
passage.text: I work at a community garden near my house. We grow all kinds of vegetables and then give them to local families who need food. I really enjoy working outside and learning about different types of plants. My grandfather used to be a gardener, so he gives me lots of useful advice on what to plant and when. The best part is seeing the plants grow week by week and knowing that we are helping people in our area who don't have enough to eat. It's a very satisfying feeling, and I've made some good friends there too.

passage.label: Chloe
passage.text: I help at a charity shop on Saturdays. I sort through clothes and books, and sometimes I work at the cash register. It's really fun because I meet so many different people and I love the friendly atmosphere. My older sister also volunteers there, so we always go together. I've made some new friends there, and we often have lunch together after work. I think it's a great way to spend my weekend because I'm doing something useful and I also get to socialize with nice people. I would recommend it to anyone.

questionGroups.instruction: For each question, choose the correct answer.

questions.prompt: Who works with a member of their family?
Who says the work can be hard?
Who gets advice from a relative?
Who has friends that sometimes help?
Who enjoys seeing the results of their work?
Who says they learned something from volunteering?

passage.text: The Williams family is full of creative people. Mrs. Williams is an art teacher at a local school, and she also paints beautiful pictures in her free time. Her husband, Mr. Williams, is a professional photographer who takes pictures for magazines and newspapers. They both encourage their children to follow their artistic interests.

Their daughter, Sophie, started drawing when she was only four years old. She loved to draw animals and people, and she would spend hours with her coloured pencils. Now, at sixteen, she is already selling her paintings online and has won several competitions. She is also taking art classes every weekend to improve her skills.

Sophie's grandmother, who was also an artist, taught her how to mix colours and use different brushes. Sophie says, 'My grandmother is my biggest inspiration. She always tells me to follow my dreams and never give up, even when things get difficult.'

Last month, Sophie had her first exhibition at a local gallery. Many people came to see her work, and she sold three paintings. Her parents were very proud of her. Mr. Williams took lots of photos of the event, and Mrs. Williams helped to arrange the exhibition. It was a very special day for the whole family.

'Sophie has a real talent,' says her grandmother. 'She works very hard and never gives up. I'm sure she will become a famous artist one day. I couldn't be happier for her, and I know she has a bright future ahead.'

questionGroups.instruction: For each question, choose the correct answer.

questions.prompt: Who taught Sophie how to mix colours?
What does Sophie's grandmother say about her?

options.label: She will become a famous artist.

passage.text: Chocolate comes from the cacao tree. For centuries, people in Central America (19) ........ used cacao beans as money and to make a drink. The drink was bitter, not sweet like today. When the Spanish came, they (20) ........ sugar to it and took it to Europe. At first, only rich people could (21) ........ chocolate because it was expensive. Later, machines made it easier to (22) ........ chocolate bars. Now, chocolate is enjoyed all over the world. In many countries, people give chocolate as (23) ........ on special days like Valentine's Day. There are many different types, from dark to white. Some people say that chocolate is (24) ........ for your heart, but it's best to eat it in small amounts.

questionGroups.instruction: For each question, choose the correct answer.

passage.text: Hi Tom, How are you? I'm writing to tell you about my new hobby. I (25) ........ started learning to cook. It's really fun!
Yesterday, I made a cake (26) ........ my mother's recipe. It was delicious. Next week, I want to make pizza. Do you want (27) ........ come and try it? We can cook together.
I also need (28) ........ buy some ingredients. Are you free on Saturday? We can go to the supermarket (29) ........ the morning.
Let me know. I (30) ........ see you soon.
